steps.title: Trace the word route
steps.content: Both examples follow the same path: an Indigenous-language word enters Spanish and then English. Cacahuatl becomes Spanish cacao before English cocoa, and Taino mahis reaches English as maize through Spanish.

steps.title: Choose the supported claim
steps.content: A and C make unsupported claims about equal numbers or equal contributions. D adds reciprocal borrowing into Indigenous languages, which the text never says. The supported point is that Spanish served as an intermediary. The answer is B.
